title: 3 dead, 1 critically hurt in La. home invasion
summary: Gunmen in dark clothes kicked down an apartment door in a troubled New Orleans-area neighborhood and opened fire, killing two children and a woman, authorities said. An 11-year-old girl was in stable condition Saturday evening after the early morning shooting, and investigators were searching for suspects, said Jefferson Parish Sheriff's spokesman Col. John Fortunato. The girl, whose name was not released, had been shot more than once. Dominique Sterling, 19, was shot in the back; her 23-month-old son, Robert Claiborne and a 6-year-old boy named Four Overstreet both were shot in the head, in bed. Investigators found marijuana and crack cocaine in the apartment, Fortunato said. Fortunato said both suspects used handguns and one wore a red bandanna, a black baseball cap and black hooded swea…

title: 5 things that help maintain bike
summary: Like any machine, a bike needs maintenance. Here are five simple things you need to do. If you do them yourself, you'll save a few bucks too. 1) CHAIN LUBRICATION: Even chains on cheap beater bikes need it. Otherwise, the links will stiffen, maybe even rust. Then they won't bend around the cogs, or they won't bend without skipping. Lubricate regularly. And, preferably, clean the bike before you do. Even a good wash with plain, old soap and water followed by a thorough rinse is better than nothing. That will wash the sand and grit out from between the lines. Then dry and lube. 2) WORN BRAKE PADS: They're designed to wear out, and they will. Check them on regular basis, because it's a bad thing -- a very, very bad thing -- when you clinch those brake levers in a death-grip hoping to avoid a …

title: Palin stands firm on consent
summary: JUNEAU -- State legislators are talking about compromising on a major abortion battle over parental consent, but Gov. Sarah Palin isn't interested in the deal. The compromise under discussion would be legislation that requires parental notification but not consent. That means parents would have to be told about their teenager's plan to have an abortion but wouldn't have to give their permission for it to happen. North Pole Republican Rep. John Coghill, with Palin's backing, is still pushing for the full version generally requiring parental consent before girls under age 17 could get an abortion. But the state Senate blocked a similar bill last year and, without the compromise, it's hard to see how that wouldn't happen again. Coghill is well aware of that, and opponents of his bill have sig…

title: Shooting of woman by troopers called justified
summary: WASILLA -- Two state reviews have deemed justified the shooting of a Wasilla woman in February by Alaska State Troopers. Debra L. Torrey, 38, was shot to the death the evening of Feb. 3 after troopers responded to a report that a woman had fired a shot inside a medical clinic on the Palmer-Wasilla Highway near Wasilla. The two reviews were done by the state's Office of Special Prosecutions and Appeals and by the Alaska Bureau of Investigation, the troopers' unit responsible for investigating major crimes. Troopers Col. Audie Holloway described the shooting as a tragedy but said Thursday that officers had no choice. "It's not something we had any option on," he said. "It was a situation we couldn't walk away (from) for her sake and the public's sake. We were stuck there, and she took the in…

title: Victim recalls events leading up to being shot 3 times
summary: When the time came for Elizabeth Rumsey to testify in the Christopher Erin Rogers murder trial Wednesday, she left a group of girlfriends in the gallery, walked up to the witness stand, hooked her hair behind her ears and settled down to business.
